title: TRAI Assesses Network Quality at Key Puducherry Medical Facilities
summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) assessed mobile network quality at key healthcare facilities in Puducherry, including JIPMER Medical College and Rajiv Gandhi Government Women and Children Hospital, during its February 2026 independent drive test. Findings covered voice and data services from Airtel, BSNL, RJIL, and VIL, crucial for operational efficiency and emergency communications. This assessment highlights the importance of reliable telecom infrastructure for supporting public health services in the region.

title: India Proposes Drugs Rules Amendment to Streamline Blood Product Viral Testing
summary: India's MoHFW seeks public comments on amending the Drugs Rules, 1945, to streamline blood product viral testing. The proposed change aims to remove duplicate testing of finished products, as pooled plasma is already rigorously screened for HIV, Hepatitis B, and C as per global pharmacopoeial standards. This aligns regulations, rationalizes testing, reduces compliance burden, and upholds patient safety.
